title: Robots Learn to Improvise - TCR 04/07/26
description:
A physical AI system reached 99% production reliability while improvising solutions outside its training data, three rival frontier labs began sharing intelligence to defend against Chinese capability extraction, a memristor chip survived 700°C with over a billion switching cycles, Iran explicitly threatened OpenAI's $30 billion Abu Dhabi data center, and OpenAI published an economic framework proposing robot taxes and a four-day workweek.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-7-2026/

Generalist's GEN-1 system achieved 99% success rates across diverse physical manipulation tasks - folding boxes, packing phones, servicing vacuums, sorting auto parts - while improvising responses to disruptions outside its training distribution, crossing a production-reliability threshold that physical AI had been approaching for months. OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google began sharing detection intelligence through the Frontier Model Forum to identify and counter systematic capability extraction by Chinese labs, marking the first coordinated industry response to the distillation governance gap that emerged publicly in February. A USC engineering team published a memristor in *Science* that operates at 700°C with over a billion switching cycles, using a tungsten-graphene interface that prevents atomic migration failure - a breakthrough in physics-based computing that could dramatically reduce AI inference energy costs by performing matrix multiplication through Ohm's Law rather than digital calculation. Iran's IRGC published satellite imagery of OpenAI's $30 billion Abu Dhabi Stargate data center alongside an explicit destruction threat, following confirmed strikes on AWS and Oracle facilities in the region. Intel disclosed that its advanced chip packaging business is approaching multi-billion-dollar annual revenue with hyperscale AI customers. OpenAI released a 13-page policy paper proposing public wealth funds, robot taxes, portable worker benefits, and a four-day workweek. And a Port Washington, Wisconsin ballot measure became the first local vote on a data center project in 2026, with at least four similar community referendums scheduled this year.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-7-2026/
